comfortably.

behavioral economics research Nobel
Prizewinning behavioral economics
research that says the more you trade as
a retail investor the worse your results
get. It's like a perfect correlation
with the more decisions you make as a
retail investor the worse your results
get. That's generally true for most
investors. institutional investors
included, but obviously institutional
investors have, you know, proven
quantitative techniques, institutional
grade risk management that can increase
the quality, the quality of the
decisions that they make in their
portfolio while also reducing the
frequency of of of bad decisions. Uh,
retail investors don't have those things
to put guardrails around their
decision-making process. So, we built
KISS to put guardrails around our
decision-making process. You know,
globally, all of us are to some degree a
retail investor. I use Kiss to manage my
entire liquid net worth. And why why do
I do that? Because it's incredibly
effective. I've not come across a better
mousetrap to help retail investors
retire on time and comfortably. So, I
built it. That's the I'm like I can't

out there that I want to put my money in

we keep marketing to you guys? I feel
bad. But anyway, so that's why Kiss
exists. Uh, and obviously KISS is
incredibly effective. If you look at a
hypothetical performance of a $100,000
portfolio, so January the 1st, 2018,
you're almost at 300 grand if you're
following KISS, as opposed to, you know,
175 grand uh if you're following the
static pie chart that is everybody's
favorite 60/40 portfolio. Kiss has an
average annual return in this rolling
out a sample back test of 26%, which is
more than two and a half times the
average annual return of um of of the
60/40 portfolio. uh and and very
comparable to the average return of uh
uh what you would experience if you were
just naked long stocks go Bitcoin 100%
all the time but no risk management just

roller coaster of being long risk assets
uh you would have a very similar
averaging return but obviously would
have a much worse uh uh draw down um if

overlays the top down and bottom up

and dynamic position sizing into the
strategy and so that ball targeting
dynamic position sizing which makes the
return you know different And better
than just being naked long stocks go
Bitcoin all the time. Uh you know we
eliminate half of the max draw down. So
Kiss's max draw down is minus 12%. It's
the only double digit max draw down the
lifetime in the side of sample back
test. The max draw down for uh naked
loan 6010 stocks gold bitcoin is minus
25%. Uh so that's more than double uh
the kisses max draw down. And by the way
you would have crashed three separate
times in a naked loan portfolio of 60
3010 stocks gold bitcoin since January
2018. Whereas if you're following KISS,
which is exposed to the exact same
assets, you would have only had one
double- digit draw down of minus 12%. So

that's the power of the institutional
grade macro management overlays that we

dispassion executed the signal in my
portfolio and obviously I'm a lot richer
as a function of that. Um and so uh
that's that's why we do what we do. It's
the markets know more than we do as an
individual investors. The market is
better at riskmanaging market risk than
all of us. We need to be humble and
dispassionate about that. Are our
signals perfect? No, of course not.
They're not going to keep you. They're
not going to sell the top, not going to
buy the bottom. They're never going to
sell the exact top. They're never going
to buy the exact bottom. But as we show
in our KISS back test, you know,
relative to a naked loan portfolio of 60
3010 stocks go Bitcoin, KISS has a
upside capture ratio of 90%, which means
you get 90% of the upside only
participating in half of the downside.
that is such a positively skewed
asymmetrically skewed distribution of
returns that quite frankly no retail
investor in their in their mind has ever
